content:
I Originally Built It to Save Time. Then Clients Started Asking If They Could Buy It.

4 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Photo by Florian Olivo on UnsplashA few years ago, my Python projects had a recurring problem.
They were technically impressive.
But commercially useless.
I had folders full of web scrapers, data processors, automation tools, and dashboards that nobody wanted to pay for.
Then one client asked a simple question after seeing a small automation tool I built:
“Can we use this too?”
That question completely changed how I approached Python development.
Instead of building projects for developers, I started building solutions for businesses.
The result?
One automation system evolved into:

• A freelance service

• A monthly subscription product

• A consulting package
All powered by a handful of Python libraries.
If you’re looking for Python tools that can help you create products people actually pay for, these are the libraries that made the…
